title: Style Footer for Consistency and Responsiveness
description: Apply styling to the footer to ensure it matches the application's design and remains responsive.
details: Add or update CSS in src/app/globals.css to style the footer. Ensure it has appropriate padding, font size, color, and background to match the existing UI. Use flexbox or other layout techniques to keep the footer at the bottom of the viewport on all screen sizes. Test for visual consistency with the rest of the application.
test_strategy: Check the footer's appearance on desktop and mobile. Ensure it does not overlap content and remains visually consistent with the app's style. Use browser dev tools to inspect CSS.

title: Validate Footer Implementation
description: Test the footer in the running application to ensure correct rendering, link behavior, and layout.
details: Run the application locally. Manually verify that the footer is present, styled correctly, and the GitHub link works as expected. Optionally, add a snapshot test for the Footer component or an end-to-end test to confirm its presence on all pages.
test_strategy: Manual verification in browser. Optionally, write a Jest snapshot test or Cypress E2E test to check for the footer's presence and correct link behavior.

title: Create Footer Component
description: Develop a reusable Footer component that displays the author's name with a link to their GitHub profile.
details: Create a new file at src/components/ui/Footer.tsx. Implement a React functional component that returns a semantic <footer> element. Inside, display the text 'RafiulM' as a hyperlink to https://github.com/RafiulM. Use target="_blank" and rel="noopener noreferrer" for the link. Ensure the component is accessible and uses semantic HTML.
test_strategy: Verify the component renders correctly in isolation. Check that the link opens in a new tab and uses proper rel attributes. Confirm accessibility with screen readers.

title: Integrate Footer into Global Layout
description: Add the Footer component to the root layout so it appears on all pages at the bottom.
details: Import the Footer component into src/app/layout.tsx. Render it inside the <body> tag, after the {children} element, to ensure it appears at the bottom of the page content. Adjust the layout structure to use flexbox (e.g., min-h-screen flex flex-col on the body) so the footer stays at the bottom without overlapping content.
test_strategy: Manually verify that the footer appears on all pages, remains at the bottom, and does not overlap or obscure main content. Resize the window and test on different viewports.
